message.content:
You are fixing ESLint warnings for RECO-800 in this NestJS repo.

TASK: Fix 5 ESLint warnings in `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts`:
- Line 26: `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` — Unnecessary conditional `??` and optional chain
- Line 65: `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` — Same pattern
- Line 108: `custom-rules/require-object-params` — function must use single object destructuring parameter

STEPS:
1. Create branch: `git checkout main && git checkout -b RECO-800-fix-eslint-style-copurchases-service`
2. Read the file around lines 26, 65, 108
3. For lines 26 and 65: remove unnecessary `??` and `?.` operators (the left-hand side is never null/undefined per TypeScript types)
4. For line 108: convert positional params to named object params and update all call sites
5. Run `npx eslint src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts` — verify 0 warnings
6. Run `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty` — verify types pass
7. Commit with `--no-verify`: message "RECO-800: fix ESLint warnings in style-copurchases.service.ts"
8. Push with `--no-verify`
9. Create PR via `gh pr create`

Add Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> to commit.
Use --no-verify for both commit and push (hooks run full test suite which times out).
Search ENTIRE codebase for call sites when changing function signatures.
NO COMMENTS in code per project rules.

Let me apply all the fixes.

content.text: Now let me also check the `paramFormatter` issue. The original paramFormatter used `([pairs]: [...]` which is a tuple (positional) format. Looking at the current file, it was changed to `({ pairs }: {...})`. But wait -- the `@LogExecution` paramFormatter receives the arguments as an array. Let me check how paramFormatter works with named params.

content.content:
12:		paramFormatter: ([{ styleId, limit, excludeIds }]: [
46:		paramFormatter: ([

content.text: OK so the existing methods at lines 12 and 46 already use `([{...}]: [...])` pattern (tuple of one object). The `bulkInsert` paramFormatter was changed to `({ pairs }: {...})` which is wrong -- it should be `([{ pairs }]: [{ ... }])` to match the LogExecution pattern. Let me re-read the current state of the file.
The paramFormatter should receive the args as a tuple (array), matching the other methods in this file. Let me fix it.

content.text: There's a TS error in the model file. Let me check it.

The model's `bulkInsert` has a syntax error -- the destructured parameter object isn't closed. It's missing the closing `}` before `)`. The type annotation `{ pairs: {...}[] }` needs to be closed with `};` then `}`. Let me look more carefully.

The issue is: `}: {` opens the type annotation, then it has `pairs: { ... }[],` with a comma instead of semicolon, and then `)` appears on line 220 which is wrong -- it should be `}` to close the type, then `)`.
